name: Crohn's Disease - Biologic WITHOUT Diagnosis (Negation / RA Contamination)
description: **Negation test for cross-indication detection.** Find patients prescribed anti-TNF biologics (infliximab, adalimumab) or ustekinumab who do NOT have a Crohn's disease diagnosis. These are typically rheumatoid arthritis or psoriasis patients on the same biologics. This is the OPPOSITE of `phekb-crohns-disease-biologic-disambig` — that test finds the intersection (Crohn's-on-biologic), this test finds the difference (non-Crohn's on the SAME biologic). Important for pharmacovigilance: if an adverse event is reported for adalimumab, you need to know whether the affected patient is on it for Crohn's, RA, or psoriasis.
prompt: Find patients prescribed Crohn's-class biologics (infliximab, adalimumab, ustekinumab) who do NOT have a Crohn's disease diagnosis. These are typically rheumatoid arthritis or psoriasis patients on the same biologics — important to identify when conducting drug safety surveillance.

prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed anti-TNF biologics (infliximab, adalimumab) or ustekinumab who do NOT have a Crohn's disease diagnosis. The expected population is mostly rheumatoid arthritis patients (anti-TNFs are first-line for RA). Use a two-query subtraction approach since FHIR does not support negative chains.
prompts.expert: I need set difference: MedicationRequest patients on biologics MINUS Condition patients with Crohn's diagnoses. FHIR R4 doesn't support `NOT EXISTS` natively. Two queries: (1) `MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|191831,327361,847083` for biologic Rx, (2) `Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|34000006,7620006,196977009,1197732001,1197733006` for Crohn's dx. Return query 1 patients MINUS query 2 patients. The remaining patients are mostly RA-on-biologics (cross-indication contamination).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Set difference (biologic Rx patients) MINUS (Crohn's-diagnosed patients). The result is the cross-indicated population (RA-on-biologic, etc).

required_codes.display: Crohn disease of colon and rectum
Crohn disease of jejunum and ileum
